teams.name: ICU
projects: Architected and implemented the infrastructure that allows generically integrating any video vendor's solution into Epic's remote monitoring application. Previously these integrations had to be done one-by-one and were extremely cost prohibitive.

teams.name: Skunkworks
teams.description: Task force that meant to advance framework and improve developer retention
projects: Updated Epic's web framework to support "hot reloading", whereby developers would be able to see their UI changes in near realtime. Previously they'd need to log out and back in to clear the browser's cache.
Improved Epic's internal version controls tools to largely eliminate the need for manually selecting the correct files for a commit, work that every developer did multiple times a day everyday.
Obviated the need for two ASP.NET-specific files for all new projects, making development simpler and easier to port to other platforms.
Dramatically improved web server cache performance in a few key areas by making those objects being cached immutable.
Helped introduce React Functional Components to Epic's existing architecture, while keeping backwards compatible with what we had before.

teams.name: Social Care
projects: Implemented and iterated on functionality that graphically indicates a patient and their relationships. Besides being functionally quite useful it also beautified an otherwise mundane screen.

teams.name: Telehealth
projects: Lead architect and developer on flagship product that allows patients to schedule video visits with their organization's partners. This both reduced patient wait times and reduced unnecessary usage of in-person facilities.

teams.name: Endocrinology
projects: Part of small team that pioneered Epic's first Endocrinology module: a tool that standardized recording and displaying insulin instructions.

teams.name: MyChart
teams.description: Team that builds the website patients use to connect to their healthcare organization
projects: Improved performance of graphing infrastructure. Prototyped an even bigger improvement that was later implemented.

teams.name: Scanning
projects: Maintained workflows around and integration with DMS vendors.

teams.name: Trending Tools
projects: Maintained Epic's tools used for displaying patient data graphically and in table form.
